title: hydrocotyle chamaemorus var. citriodora
intro: Hydrocotyle chamaemorus var. citriodora is a variant of the Winter-cress or Spring-bitter-cress plant, which is a type of aquatic or semi-aquatic plant that belongs to the family Hydrocotylaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, due to the presence of compounds like limonene and citral. The health benefits of Hydrocotyle chamaemorus var. citriodora include relief from skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and anti-cancer agent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it can also be used topically as a cream or ointment to apply directly to affected areas.
